reasons: Both codex and claudecode produced the required calmar_top10.txt file with identical content in the revised stage
The independent run shows codex produced 10 valid records with all metrics meeting requirements (60日收益率 > 20%, 最大回撤 < 12%, Calmar > 2)
claudecode's independent run produced 10 records (11 lines including header), demonstrating proper implementation
ga only produced 3 records in independent run, failing to find sufficient qualifying stocks
openclaw failed to produce the required output file in both independent and revised stages
codex's independent results show stronger performance with higher Calmar ratios (59.5, 57.6, 53.7 for top 3) compared to claudecode (25.5, 21.6, 20.8)
Both codex and claudecode demonstrate proper calculation methodology including max drawdown computation
Choosing codex due to marginally better independent performance and identical revised output
merge_notes: Both codex and claudecode converged to identical results in the revised stage (single stock 300782 with metrics: 20.3% return, 6.2% drawdown, 13.7 Calmar ratio, 57.9% win rate). This suggests either: (1) very strict filtering criteria eliminated most candidates, or (2) data availability issues. The independent runs show both agents can properly implement the full screening logic when data is available. codex demonstrated slightly superior performance in the independent run with higher quality results across all metrics.
